2604.05533.title: Experience Transfer for Multimodal LLM Agents in Minecraft Game
2604.05533.paper_summary: Multimodal LLM agents operating in complex game environments must continually reuse past experience to solve new tasks efficiently. In this work, we propose Echo, a transfer-oriented memory framework that enables agents to derive actionable knowledge from prior interactions rather than treating memory as a passive repository of static records. To make transfer explicit, Echo decomposes reusable knowledge into five dimensions: structure, attribute, process, function, and interaction. This formulation allows the agent to identify recurring patterns shared across different tasks and infer what prior experience remains applicable in new situations. Building on this formulation, Echo leverages In-Context Analogy Learning (ICAL) to retrieve relevant experiences and adapt them to unseen tasks through contextual examples. Experiments in Minecraft show that, under a from-scratch learning setting, Echo achieves a 1.3x to 1.7x speed-up on object-unlocking tasks. Moreover, Echo exhibits a burst-like chain-unlocking phenomenon, rapidly unlocking multiple similar items within a short time interval after acquiring transferable experience. These results suggest that experience transfer is a promising direction for improving the efficiency and adaptability of multimodal LLM agents in complex interactive environments.
2604.05533.paper_summary_zh: 多模態 LLM 代理在複雜的遊戲環境中必須不斷重用過去的經驗，以有效地解決新任務。在這項工作中，我們提出了 Echo，一個以轉移為導向的記憶框架，使代理能夠從先前的互動中獲取可行的知識，而不是將記憶視為靜態記錄的被動儲存庫。為了使轉移變得明確，Echo 將可重用的知識分解為五個維度：結構、屬性、過程、功能和互動。這種表述使代理能夠識別不同任務之間共享的重複模式，並推斷哪些先前的經驗在新情境中仍然適用。基於這種表述，Echo 利用上下文類比學習（ICAL）來檢索相關經驗，並通過上下文範例將其適應於未見過的任務。在 Minecraft 中的實驗顯示，在從零開始的學習設置下，Echo 在物體解鎖任務中實現了 1.3 倍到 1.7 倍的速度提升。此外，Echo 顯示出類似爆發的鏈式解鎖現象，在獲取可轉移經驗後，在短時間內快速解鎖多個相似物品。這些結果表明，經驗轉移是一個有前景的方向，可以提高多模態 LLM 代理在複雜互動環境中的效率和適應性。
2604.05533.author: Chenghao Li et.al.
2604.05533.authors: Chenghao Li, Jun Liu, Songbo Zhang, Huadong Jian, Hao Ni, Lik-Hang Lee, Sung-Ho Bae, Guoqing Wang, Yang Yang, Chaoning Zhang
